<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井城]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11432148]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The similarly named castle in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]'s [[:en:Ena City|Ena City]]|{{ill|Ōi Castle (Mino Province)|ja|大井城 (美濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Castle (Mino Province)|1=Oi Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118066}}}} {{Infobox Japanese castle | name = Ōi Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | alias = Ishinarabi Castle, Ōjō, Kuroiwa Castle | structure = Hirayama-style castle | tower_struct = | builders = {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}? | build_year = [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221) | rulers = {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}}, {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, {{ill|Ōi Awa-maru|ja|大井安房丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Awa-maru|1=Ōi Awamaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22124423}}, among others | destroyed_year = [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) | remains = Kuruwa (enclosures) | cultural_asset = Nagano Prefecture Historic Site<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Historic Sites|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/13ken-siseki.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref> | location=|latd=36|latm=16|lats=29.8 | longd=138|longm=28|longs=55.9 | map=Japan Nagano | label=Ōi Castle | latitude_deg=36|latitude_min=16|latitude_sec=29.8 | longitude_deg=138|longitude_min=28|longitude_sec=55.9 | icon=Japanese castle ruins | label_position=bottom }}
'''Ōi Castle''' (大井城, ''Ōi-jō'') was a [[:en:Japanese castle|Japanese castle]] located in [[Iwamurada]], [[Saku City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a Nagano Prefecture-designated [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] and was the seat of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as deputies to the [[Governor of Shinano Province]]. The castle was also known as '''Iwamurada Residence''' (岩村田館).
== History ==
Ōi Castle was located in northeastern Saku City, along the [[Yukawa River (Nagano)|Yukawa]]'s cliffs. It consisted of three fortifications: Ishinarabi Castle (石並城), Ōjō (王城), and Kuroiwa Castle (黒岩城). According to the ''[[Shirin Danzō]]'', the castle was established by {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a branch of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] descending from [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagachika]]. From its founding in [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221) until its fall in [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), the Ōi clan controlled northeastern Saku District from this base.
In [[Kōan (era)|Kōan]] 2 (1279), the second lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}} donated a temple bell to the {{ill|Shinzenkō-ji|ja|新善光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenkō-ji|1=Shinzenkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501510}} in [[:en:Naruse|Naruse]], Saku. The exact location of the manor depicted in the ''Illustrated Biography of Ippen'' remains uncertain.
During the [[:en:Kemmu Restoration|Kemmu Restoration]] in [[Kemmu (era)|Kemmu]] 2 (1335), the fourth lord {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}} supported the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], joining the Northern Court. He clashed with the Southern Court forces, including {{ill|Kutsuna Shigekiyo|ja|忽那重清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuna Shigekiyo|1=Kutsuna Shigekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56253681}}, who were advancing toward [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]] along the Eastern Mountain Road. Despite several days of fierce resistance, the castle eventually fell, as recorded in documents like ''Battle of Ōi Manor'' and ''Kutsuna Papers''.
The castle was later rebuilt. The fifth lord, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, served as Deputy Governor of Shinano Province. His son, the sixth lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, mediated during the [[Ōtō Rebellion]] in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 7 (1400), aiding the defeated [[:en:Ogasawara Nagahide|Ogasawara Nagahide]].
Under the seventh lord {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, the clan reached its zenith. The Ōi castle town flourished, with over 6,000 households, surpassing the provincial capital ([[:en:Matsumoto City|Matsumoto]]). Mochimitsu's reign saw vast territorial control, including 60,000 ''koku'' in northern Saku and estates in [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke]] and [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi]]. He reportedly led 1,000 troops to Kyoto.
In [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] 10 (1438), Mochimitsu sheltered {{ill|Eijū ōmaru|ja|永寿王丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eijū ōmaru|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the orphaned son of [[:en:Ashikaga Mochiuji|Ashikaga Mochiuji]], at {{ill|An'yō-ji|ja|安養寺 (佐久市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'yō-ji|1=An'yō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043045}} during the [[Eikyō Rebellion]].
Subsequent lords faced difficulties. In [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), {{ill|Murakami Masakiyo|ja|村上政清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Masakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} attacked Ōi Castle, which was set ablaze, marking the end of the Ōi main family’s 260-year rule.
Though the main line perished, branches like the {{ill|Iwao clan|ja|岩尾氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwao clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Aiki clan|ja|相木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} persisted. {{ill|Ōi Genkei|ja|大井玄慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Genkei|1=Ōi Haruyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920475}} inherited the castle but was ousted by [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] in [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 12 (1543). Later, during the [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] era, {{ill|Ōi Gakujo|ja|大井雅楽助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Gakujo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrendered the castle to {{ill|Yoda Nobushige|ja|依田信蕃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoda Nobushige|1=Yoda Nobushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385519}}, a retainer of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]].
Today, little remains of the castle. The main enclosure is a public park, home to a giant [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]] designated a prefectural natural monument<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Natural Monuments|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/15ken-tennenkinenbutu.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井城]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11432148]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The similarly named castle in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]'s [[:en:Ena City|Ena City]]|{{ill|Ōi Castle (Mino Province)|ja|大井城 (美濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Castle (Mino Province)|1=Oi Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118066}}}} {{Infobox Japanese castle | name = Ōi Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | alias = Ishinarabi Castle, Ōjō, Kuroiwa Castle | structure = Hirayama-style castle | tower_struct = | builders = {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}? | build_year = [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221) | rulers = {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}}, {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, {{ill|Ōi Awa-maru|ja|大井安房丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Awa-maru|1=Ōi Awamaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22124423}}, among others | destroyed_year = [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) | remains = Kuruwa (enclosures) | cultural_asset = Nagano Prefecture Historic Site<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Historic Sites|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/13ken-siseki.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref> | location=|latd=36|latm=16|lats=29.8 | longd=138|longm=28|longs=55.9 | map=Japan Nagano | label=Ōi Castle | latitude_deg=36|latitude_min=16|latitude_sec=29.8 | longitude_deg=138|longitude_min=28|longitude_sec=55.9 | icon=Japanese castle ruins | label_position=bottom }}
'''Ōi Castle''' (大井城, ''Ōi-jō'') was a [[:en:Japanese castle|Japanese castle]] located in [[Iwamurada]], [[Saku City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a Nagano Prefecture-designated [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] and was the seat of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as deputies to the [[Governor of Shinano Province]]. The castle was also known as '''Iwamurada Residence''' (岩村田館).
== History ==
Ōi Castle was located in northeastern Saku City, along the [[Yukawa River (Nagano)|Yukawa]]'s cliffs. It consisted of three fortifications: Ishinarabi Castle (石並城), Ōjō (王城), and Kuroiwa Castle (黒岩城). According to the ''[[Shirin Danzō]]'', the castle was established by {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a branch of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] descending from [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagachika]]. From its founding in [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221) until its fall in [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), the Ōi clan controlled northeastern Saku District from this base.
In [[Kōan (era)|Kōan]] 2 (1279), the second lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}} donated a temple bell to the {{ill|Shinzenkō-ji|ja|新善光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenkō-ji|1=Shinzenkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501510}} in [[:en:Naruse|Naruse]], Saku. The exact location of the manor depicted in the ''Illustrated Biography of Ippen'' remains uncertain.
During the [[:en:Kemmu Restoration|Kemmu Restoration]] in [[Kemmu (era)|Kemmu]] 2 (1335), the fourth lord {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}} supported the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], joining the Northern Court. He clashed with the Southern Court forces, including {{ill|Kutsuna Shigekiyo|ja|忽那重清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuna Shigekiyo|1=Kutsuna Shigekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56253681}}, who were advancing toward [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]] along the Eastern Mountain Road. Despite several days of fierce resistance, the castle eventually fell, as recorded in documents like ''Battle of Ōi Manor'' and ''Kutsuna Papers''.
The castle was later rebuilt. The fifth lord, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, served as Deputy Governor of Shinano Province. His son, the sixth lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, mediated during the [[Ōtō Rebellion]] in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 7 (1400), aiding the defeated [[:en:Ogasawara Nagahide|Ogasawara Nagahide]].
Under the seventh lord {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, the clan reached its zenith. The Ōi castle town flourished, with over 6,000 households, surpassing the provincial capital ([[:en:Matsumoto City|Matsumoto]]). Mochimitsu's reign saw vast territorial control, including 60,000 ''koku'' in northern Saku and estates in [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke]] and [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi]]. He reportedly led 1,000 troops to Kyoto.
In [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] 10 (1438), Mochimitsu sheltered {{ill|Eijū ōmaru|ja|永寿王丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eijū ōmaru|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the orphaned son of [[:en:Ashikaga Mochiuji|Ashikaga Mochiuji]], at {{ill|An'yō-ji|ja|安養寺 (佐久市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'yō-ji|1=An'yō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043045}} during the [[Eikyō Rebellion]].
Subsequent lords faced difficulties. In [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), {{ill|Murakami Masakiyo|ja|村上政清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Masakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} attacked Ōi Castle, which was set ablaze, marking the end of the Ōi main family’s 260-year rule.
Though the main line perished, branches like the {{ill|Iwao clan|ja|岩尾氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwao clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Aiki clan|ja|相木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} persisted. {{ill|Ōi Genkei|ja|大井玄慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Genkei|1=Ōi Haruyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920475}} inherited the castle but was ousted by [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] in [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 12 (1543). Later, during the [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] era, {{ill|Ōi Gakujo|ja|大井雅楽助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Gakujo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrendered the castle to {{ill|Yoda Nobushige|ja|依田信蕃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoda Nobushige|1=Yoda Nobushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385519}}, a retainer of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]].
Today, little remains of the castle. The main enclosure is a public park, home to a giant [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]] designated a prefectural natural monument<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Natural Monuments|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/15ken-tennenkinenbutu.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井城]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11432148]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The similarly named castle in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]'s [[:en:Ena City|Ena City]]|{{ill|Ōi Castle (Mino Province)|ja|大井城 (美濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Castle (Mino Province)|1=Oi Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118066}}}} {{Infobox Japanese castle | name = Ōi Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | alias = Ishinarabi Castle, Ōjō, Kuroiwa Castle | structure = Hirayama-style castle | tower_struct = | builders = {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}? | build_year = [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221) | rulers = {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}}, {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, {{ill|Ōi Awa-maru|ja|大井安房丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Awa-maru|1=Ōi Awamaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22124423}}, among others | destroyed_year = [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) | remains = Kuruwa (enclosures) | cultural_asset = Nagano Prefecture Historic Site<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Historic Sites|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/13ken-siseki.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref> | location=|latd=36|latm=16|lats=29.8 | longd=138|longm=28|longs=55.9 | map=Japan Nagano | label=Ōi Castle | latitude_deg=36|latitude_min=16|latitude_sec=29.8 | longitude_deg=138|longitude_min=28|longitude_sec=55.9 | icon=Japanese castle ruins | label_position=bottom }}
'''Ōi Castle''' (大井城, ''Ōi-jō'') was a [[:en:Japanese castle|Japanese castle]] located in [[Iwamurada]], [[Saku City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a Nagano Prefecture-designated [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] and was the seat of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as deputies to the [[Governor of Shinano Province]]. The castle was also known as '''Iwamurada Residence''' (岩村田館).
== History ==
Ōi Castle was located in northeastern Saku City, along the [[Yukawa River (Nagano)|Yukawa]]'s cliffs. It consisted of three fortifications: Ishinarabi Castle (石並城), Ōjō (王城), and Kuroiwa Castle (黒岩城). According to the ''[[Shirin Danzō]]'', the castle was established by {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a branch of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] descending from [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagachika]]. From its founding in [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221) until its fall in [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), the Ōi clan controlled northeastern Saku District from this base.
In [[Kōan (era)|Kōan]] 2 (1279), the second lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}} donated a temple bell to the {{ill|Shinzenkō-ji|ja|新善光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenkō-ji|1=Shinzenkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501510}} in [[:en:Naruse|Naruse]], Saku. The exact location of the manor depicted in the ''Illustrated Biography of Ippen'' remains uncertain.
During the [[:en:Kemmu Restoration|Kemmu Restoration]] in [[Kemmu (era)|Kemmu]] 2 (1335), the fourth lord {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}} supported the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], joining the Northern Court. He clashed with the Southern Court forces, including {{ill|Kutsuna Shigekiyo|ja|忽那重清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuna Shigekiyo|1=Kutsuna Shigekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56253681}}, who were advancing toward [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]] along the Eastern Mountain Road. Despite several days of fierce resistance, the castle eventually fell, as recorded in documents like ''Battle of Ōi Manor'' and ''Kutsuna Papers''.
The castle was later rebuilt. The fifth lord, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, served as Deputy Governor of Shinano Province. His son, the sixth lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, mediated during the [[Ōtō Rebellion]] in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 7 (1400), aiding the defeated [[:en:Ogasawara Nagahide|Ogasawara Nagahide]].
Under the seventh lord {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, the clan reached its zenith. The Ōi castle town flourished, with over 6,000 households, surpassing the provincial capital ([[:en:Matsumoto City|Matsumoto]]). Mochimitsu's reign saw vast territorial control, including 60,000 ''koku'' in northern Saku and estates in [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke]] and [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi]]. He reportedly led 1,000 troops to Kyoto.
In [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] 10 (1438), Mochimitsu sheltered {{ill|Eijū ōmaru|ja|永寿王丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eijū ōmaru|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the orphaned son of [[:en:Ashikaga Mochiuji|Ashikaga Mochiuji]], at {{ill|An'yō-ji|ja|安養寺 (佐久市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'yō-ji|1=An'yō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043045}} during the [[Eikyō Rebellion]].
Subsequent lords faced difficulties. In [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), {{ill|Murakami Masakiyo|ja|村上政清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Masakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} attacked Ōi Castle, which was set ablaze, marking the end of the Ōi main family’s 260-year rule.
Though the main line perished, branches like the {{ill|Iwao clan|ja|岩尾氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwao clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Aiki clan|ja|相木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} persisted. {{ill|Ōi Genkei|ja|大井玄慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Genkei|1=Ōi Haruyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920475}} inherited the castle but was ousted by [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] in [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 12 (1543). Later, during the [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] era, {{ill|Ōi Gakujo|ja|大井雅楽助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Gakujo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrendered the castle to {{ill|Yoda Nobushige|ja|依田信蕃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoda Nobushige|1=Yoda Nobushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385519}}, a retainer of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]].
Today, little remains of the castle. The main enclosure is a public park, home to a giant [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]] designated a prefectural natural monument<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Natural Monuments|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/15ken-tennenkinenbutu.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
== Bibliography == * ''{{ill|Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|ja|日本城郭大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|1=Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507767}}'' Vol. 8, Nagano-Yamanashi, 1980 * Masaharu Sasamoto, ''The Two Battles of Kawanakajima'', 1998 * Hisako Tanabe, ''Uesugi Norinaga'', 1999 * Yuki Sakata, ''Kantō Kubō Ashikaga Nariuji'', 2004 * Shinobu Ōi, ''Tales of the Ōi Clan'', 2005 * Toshio Ōi, ''Chronicles of the Iwao Family'' (Modern Japanese Translation), 1996
== External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www1.ocn.ne.jp/~oomi/sakugun.html|title=History of the Saku Area – Prosperity of Iwamurada|date=2015-07-02}} * {{Wayback|url=http://rarememory.justhpbs.jp/ooi/ooi.htm|title=Rise and Fall of the Ōi Clan|date=2012-05-02}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ju8t-hnm/Shiro/TokaiKoshin/Nagano/Ooi/index.html|title=Ōi Castle: Buried Fortresses|date=2016-03-04}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井城]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11432148]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The similarly named castle in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]'s [[:en:Ena City|Ena City]]|{{ill|Ōi Castle (Mino Province)|ja|大井城 (美濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Castle (Mino Province)|1=Oi Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118066}}}} {{Infobox Japanese castle | name = Ōi Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | alias = Ishinarabi Castle, Ōjō, Kuroiwa Castle | structure = Hirayama-style castle | tower_struct = | builders = {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}? | build_year = [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221) | rulers = {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}}, {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, {{ill|Ōi Awa-maru|ja|大井安房丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Awa-maru|1=Ōi Awamaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22124423}}, among others | destroyed_year = [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) | remains = Kuruwa (enclosures) | cultural_asset = Nagano Prefecture Historic Site<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Historic Sites|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/13ken-siseki.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref> | location=|latd=36|latm=16|lats=29.8 | longd=138|longm=28|longs=55.9 | map=Japan Nagano | label=Ōi Castle | latitude_deg=36|latitude_min=16|latitude_sec=29.8 | longitude_deg=138|longitude_min=28|longitude_sec=55.9 | icon=Japanese castle ruins | label_position=bottom }}
'''Ōi Castle''' (大井城, ''Ōi-jō'') was a [[:en:Japanese castle|Japanese castle]] located in [[Iwamurada]], [[Saku City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a Nagano Prefecture-designated [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] and was the seat of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as deputies to the [[Governor of Shinano Province]]. The castle was also known as '''Iwamurada Residence''' (岩村田館).
== History ==
Ōi Castle was located in northeastern Saku City, along the [[Yukawa River (Nagano)|Yukawa]]'s cliffs. It consisted of three fortifications: Ishinarabi Castle (石並城), Ōjō (王城), and Kuroiwa Castle (黒岩城). According to the ''[[Shirin Danzō]]'', the castle was established by {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a branch of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] descending from [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagachika]]. From its founding in [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221) until its fall in [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), the Ōi clan controlled northeastern Saku District from this base.
In [[Kōan (era)|Kōan]] 2 (1279), the second lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}} donated a temple bell to the {{ill|Shinzenkō-ji|ja|新善光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenkō-ji|1=Shinzenkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501510}} in [[:en:Naruse|Naruse]], Saku. The exact location of the manor depicted in the ''Illustrated Biography of Ippen'' remains uncertain.
During the [[:en:Kemmu Restoration|Kemmu Restoration]] in [[Kemmu (era)|Kemmu]] 2 (1335), the fourth lord {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}} supported the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], joining the Northern Court. He clashed with the Southern Court forces, including {{ill|Kutsuna Shigekiyo|ja|忽那重清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuna Shigekiyo|1=Kutsuna Shigekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56253681}}, who were advancing toward [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]] along the Eastern Mountain Road. Despite several days of fierce resistance, the castle eventually fell, as recorded in documents like ''Battle of Ōi Manor'' and ''Kutsuna Papers''.
The castle was later rebuilt. The fifth lord, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, served as Deputy Governor of Shinano Province. His son, the sixth lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, mediated during the [[Ōtō Rebellion]] in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 7 (1400), aiding the defeated [[:en:Ogasawara Nagahide|Ogasawara Nagahide]].
Under the seventh lord {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, the clan reached its zenith. The Ōi castle town flourished, with over 6,000 households, surpassing the provincial capital ([[:en:Matsumoto City|Matsumoto]]). Mochimitsu's reign saw vast territorial control, including 60,000 ''koku'' in northern Saku and estates in [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke]] and [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi]]. He reportedly led 1,000 troops to Kyoto.
In [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] 10 (1438), Mochimitsu sheltered {{ill|Eijū ōmaru|ja|永寿王丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eijū ōmaru|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the orphaned son of [[:en:Ashikaga Mochiuji|Ashikaga Mochiuji]], at {{ill|An'yō-ji|ja|安養寺 (佐久市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'yō-ji|1=An'yō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043045}} during the [[Eikyō Rebellion]].
Subsequent lords faced difficulties. In [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), {{ill|Murakami Masakiyo|ja|村上政清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Masakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} attacked Ōi Castle, which was set ablaze, marking the end of the Ōi main family’s 260-year rule.
Though the main line perished, branches like the {{ill|Iwao clan|ja|岩尾氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwao clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Aiki clan|ja|相木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} persisted. {{ill|Ōi Genkei|ja|大井玄慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Genkei|1=Ōi Haruyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920475}} inherited the castle but was ousted by [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] in [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 12 (1543). Later, during the [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] era, {{ill|Ōi Gakujo|ja|大井雅楽助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Gakujo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrendered the castle to {{ill|Yoda Nobushige|ja|依田信蕃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoda Nobushige|1=Yoda Nobushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385519}}, a retainer of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]].
Today, little remains of the castle. The main enclosure is a public park, home to a giant [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]] designated a prefectural natural monument<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Natural Monuments|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/15ken-tennenkinenbutu.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
== Bibliography == * ''{{ill|Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|ja|日本城郭大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|1=Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507767}}'' Vol. 8, Nagano-Yamanashi, 1980 * Masaharu Sasamoto, ''The Two Battles of Kawanakajima'', 1998 * Hisako Tanabe, ''Uesugi Norinaga'', 1999 * Yuki Sakata, ''Kantō Kubō Ashikaga Nariuji'', 2004 * Shinobu Ōi, ''Tales of the Ōi Clan'', 2005 * Toshio Ōi, ''Chronicles of the Iwao Family'' (Modern Japanese Translation), 1996
== External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www1.ocn.ne.jp/~oomi/sakugun.html|title=History of the Saku Area – Prosperity of Iwamurada|date=2015-07-02}} * {{Wayback|url=http://rarememory.justhpbs.jp/ooi/ooi.htm|title=Rise and Fall of the Ōi Clan|date=2012-05-02}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ju8t-hnm/Shiro/TokaiKoshin/Nagano/Ooi/index.html|title=Ōi Castle: Buried Fortresses|date=2016-03-04}}

| build_year = [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221) | rulers = {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}}, {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, {{ill|Ōi Awa-maru|ja|大井安房丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Awa-maru|1=Ōi Awamaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22124423}}, among others | destroyed_year = [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) | remains = Kuruwa (enclosures) | cultural_asset = Nagano Prefecture Historic Site<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Historic Sites|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/13ken-siseki.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref> | location=|latd=36|latm=16|lats=29.8 | longd=138|longm=28|longs=55.9 | map=Japan Nagano | label=Ōi Castle | latitude_deg=36|latitude_min=16|latitude_sec=29.8 | longitude_deg=138|longitude_min=28|longitude_sec=55.9 | icon=Japanese castle ruins | label_position=bottom }}
'''Ōi Castle''' (大井城, ''Ōi-jō'') was a [[:en:Japanese castle|Japanese castle]] located in [[Iwamurada]], [[Saku City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a Nagano Prefecture-designated [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] and was the seat of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as deputies to the [[Governor of Shinano Province]]. The castle was also known as '''Iwamurada Residence''' (岩村田館).
== History ==
Ōi Castle was located in northeastern Saku City, along the [[Yukawa River (Nagano)|Yukawa]]'s cliffs. It consisted of three fortifications: Ishinarabi Castle (石並城), Ōjō (王城), and Kuroiwa Castle (黒岩城). According to the ''[[Shirin Danzō]]'', the castle was established by {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a branch of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] descending from [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagachika]]. From its founding in [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221) until its fall in [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), the Ōi clan controlled northeastern Saku District from this base.
In [[Kōan (era)|Kōan]] 2 (1279), the second lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}} donated a temple bell to the {{ill|Shinzenkō-ji|ja|新善光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenkō-ji|1=Shinzenkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501510}} in [[:en:Naruse|Naruse]], Saku. The exact location of the manor depicted in the ''Illustrated Biography of Ippen'' remains uncertain.
During the [[:en:Kemmu Restoration|Kemmu Restoration]] in [[Kemmu (era)|Kemmu]] 2 (1335), the fourth lord {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}} supported the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], joining the Northern Court. He clashed with the Southern Court forces, including {{ill|Kutsuna Shigekiyo|ja|忽那重清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuna Shigekiyo|1=Kutsuna Shigekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56253681}}, who were advancing toward [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]] along the Eastern Mountain Road. Despite several days of fierce resistance, the castle eventually fell, as recorded in documents like ''Battle of Ōi Manor'' and ''Kutsuna Papers''.
The castle was later rebuilt. The fifth lord, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, served as Deputy Governor of Shinano Province. His son, the sixth lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, mediated during the [[Ōtō Rebellion]] in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 7 (1400), aiding the defeated [[:en:Ogasawara Nagahide|Ogasawara Nagahide]].
Under the seventh lord {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, the clan reached its zenith. The Ōi castle town flourished, with over 6,000 households, surpassing the provincial capital ([[:en:Matsumoto City|Matsumoto]]). Mochimitsu's reign saw vast territorial control, including 60,000 ''koku'' in northern Saku and estates in [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke]] and [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi]]. He reportedly led 1,000 troops to Kyoto.
In [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] 10 (1438), Mochimitsu sheltered {{ill|Eijū ōmaru|ja|永寿王丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eijū ōmaru|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the orphaned son of [[:en:Ashikaga Mochiuji|Ashikaga Mochiuji]], at {{ill|An'yō-ji|ja|安養寺 (佐久市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'yō-ji|1=An'yō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043045}} during the [[Eikyō Rebellion]].
Subsequent lords faced difficulties. In [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), {{ill|Murakami Masakiyo|ja|村上政清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Masakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} attacked Ōi Castle, which was set ablaze, marking the end of the Ōi main family’s 260-year rule.
Though the main line perished, branches like the {{ill|Iwao clan|ja|岩尾氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwao clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Aiki clan|ja|相木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} persisted. {{ill|Ōi Genkei|ja|大井玄慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Genkei|1=Ōi Haruyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920475}} inherited the castle but was ousted by [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] in [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 12 (1543). Later, during the [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] era, {{ill|Ōi Gakujo|ja|大井雅楽助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Gakujo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrendered the castle to {{ill|Yoda Nobushige|ja|依田信蕃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoda Nobushige|1=Yoda Nobushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385519}}, a retainer of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]].
Today, little remains of the castle. The main enclosure is a public park, home to a giant [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]] designated a prefectural natural monument<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Natural Monuments|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/15ken-tennenkinenbutu.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
== Bibliography == * ''{{ill|Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|ja|日本城郭大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|1=Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507767}}'' Vol. 8, Nagano-Yamanashi, 1980 * Masaharu Sasamoto, ''The Two Battles of Kawanakajima'', 1998 * Hisako Tanabe, ''Uesugi Norinaga'', 1999 * Yuki Sakata, ''Kantō Kubō Ashikaga Nariuji'', 2004 * Shinobu Ōi, ''Tales of the Ōi Clan'', 2005 * Toshio Ōi, ''Chronicles of the Iwao Family'' (Modern Japanese Translation), 1996
== External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www1.ocn.ne.jp/~oomi/sakugun.html|title=History of the Saku Area – Prosperity of Iwamurada|date=2015-07-02}} * {{Wayback|url=http://rarememory.justhpbs.jp/ooi/ooi.htm|title=Rise and Fall of the Ōi Clan|date=2012-05-02}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ju8t-hnm/Shiro/TokaiKoshin/Nagano/Ooi/index.html|title=Ōi Castle: Buried Fortresses|date=2016-03-04}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井城]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井城]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井城]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432148]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Otheruses||The similarly named castle in [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]'s [[:en:Ena City|Ena City]]|{{ill|Ōi Castle (Mino Province)|ja|大井城 (美濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Castle (Mino Province)|1=Oi Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22118066}}}} {{Infobox Japanese castle | name = Ōi Castle | pref = Nagano Prefecture | alias = Ishinarabi Castle, Ōjō, Kuroiwa Castle | structure = Hirayama-style castle | tower_struct = | builders = {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}? | build_year = [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221) | rulers = {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}}, {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, {{ill|Ōi Awa-maru|ja|大井安房丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Awa-maru|1=Ōi Awamaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22124423}}, among others | destroyed_year = [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) | remains = Kuruwa (enclosures) | cultural_asset = Nagano Prefecture Historic Site<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Historic Sites|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/13ken-siseki.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref> | location=|latd=36|latm=16|lats=29.8 | longd=138|longm=28|longs=55.9 | map=Japan Nagano | label=Ōi Castle | latitude_deg=36|latitude_min=16|latitude_sec=29.8 | longitude_deg=138|longitude_min=28|longitude_sec=55.9 | icon=Japanese castle ruins | label_position=bottom }}
'''Ōi Castle''' (大井城, ''Ōi-jō'') was a [[:en:Japanese castle|Japanese castle]] located in [[Iwamurada]], [[Saku City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a Nagano Prefecture-designated [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] and was the seat of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as deputies to the [[Governor of Shinano Province]]. The castle was also known as '''Iwamurada Residence''' (岩村田館).
== History ==
Ōi Castle was located in northeastern Saku City, along the [[Yukawa River (Nagano)|Yukawa]]'s cliffs. It consisted of three fortifications: Ishinarabi Castle (石並城), Ōjō (王城), and Kuroiwa Castle (黒岩城). According to the ''[[Shirin Danzō]]'', the castle was established by {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a branch of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] descending from [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagachika]]. From its founding in [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221) until its fall in [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), the Ōi clan controlled northeastern Saku District from this base.
In [[Kōan (era)|Kōan]] 2 (1279), the second lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}} donated a temple bell to the {{ill|Shinzenkō-ji|ja|新善光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenkō-ji|1=Shinzenkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501510}} in [[:en:Naruse|Naruse]], Saku. The exact location of the manor depicted in the ''Illustrated Biography of Ippen'' remains uncertain.
During the [[:en:Kemmu Restoration|Kemmu Restoration]] in [[Kemmu (era)|Kemmu]] 2 (1335), the fourth lord {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}} supported the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], joining the Northern Court. He clashed with the Southern Court forces, including {{ill|Kutsuna Shigekiyo|ja|忽那重清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuna Shigekiyo|1=Kutsuna Shigekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56253681}}, who were advancing toward [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]] along the Eastern Mountain Road. Despite several days of fierce resistance, the castle eventually fell, as recorded in documents like ''Battle of Ōi Manor'' and ''Kutsuna Papers''.
The castle was later rebuilt. The fifth lord, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, served as Deputy Governor of Shinano Province. His son, the sixth lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, mediated during the [[Ōtō Rebellion]] in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 7 (1400), aiding the defeated [[:en:Ogasawara Nagahide|Ogasawara Nagahide]].
Under the seventh lord {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, the clan reached its zenith. The Ōi castle town flourished, with over 6,000 households, surpassing the provincial capital ([[:en:Matsumoto City|Matsumoto]]). Mochimitsu's reign saw vast territorial control, including 60,000 ''koku'' in northern Saku and estates in [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke]] and [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi]]. He reportedly led 1,000 troops to Kyoto.
In [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] 10 (1438), Mochimitsu sheltered {{ill|Eijū ōmaru|ja|永寿王丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eijū ōmaru|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the orphaned son of [[:en:Ashikaga Mochiuji|Ashikaga Mochiuji]], at {{ill|An'yō-ji|ja|安養寺 (佐久市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'yō-ji|1=An'yō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043045}} during the [[Eikyō Rebellion]].
Subsequent lords faced difficulties. In [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), {{ill|Murakami Masakiyo|ja|村上政清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Masakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} attacked Ōi Castle, which was set ablaze, marking the end of the Ōi main family’s 260-year rule.
Though the main line perished, branches like the {{ill|Iwao clan|ja|岩尾氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwao clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Aiki clan|ja|相木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} persisted. {{ill|Ōi Genkei|ja|大井玄慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Genkei|1=Ōi Haruyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920475}} inherited the castle but was ousted by [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] in [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 12 (1543). Later, during the [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] era, {{ill|Ōi Gakujo|ja|大井雅楽助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Gakujo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrendered the castle to {{ill|Yoda Nobushige|ja|依田信蕃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoda Nobushige|1=Yoda Nobushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385519}}, a retainer of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]].
Today, little remains of the castle. The main enclosure is a public park, home to a giant [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]] designated a prefectural natural monument<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Natural Monuments|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/15ken-tennenkinenbutu.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
== Bibliography == * ''{{ill|Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|ja|日本城郭大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|1=Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507767}}'' Vol. 8, Nagano-Yamanashi, 1980 * Masaharu Sasamoto, ''The Two Battles of Kawanakajima'', 1998 * Hisako Tanabe, ''Uesugi Norinaga'', 1999 * Yuki Sakata, ''Kantō Kubō Ashikaga Nariuji'', 2004 * Shinobu Ōi, ''Tales of the Ōi Clan'', 2005 * Toshio Ōi, ''Chronicles of the Iwao Family'' (Modern Japanese Translation), 1996
== External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www1.ocn.ne.jp/~oomi/sakugun.html|title=History of the Saku Area – Prosperity of Iwamurada|date=2015-07-02}} * {{Wayback|url=http://rarememory.justhpbs.jp/ooi/ooi.htm|title=Rise and Fall of the Ōi Clan|date=2012-05-02}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ju8t-hnm/Shiro/TokaiKoshin/Nagano/Ooi/index.html|title=Ōi Castle: Buried Fortresses|date=2016-03-04}}

| build_year = [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221) | rulers = {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}}, {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, {{ill|Ōi Awa-maru|ja|大井安房丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Awa-maru|1=Ōi Awamaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22124423}}, among others | destroyed_year = [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) | remains = Kuruwa (enclosures) | cultural_asset = Nagano Prefecture Historic Site<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Historic Sites|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/13ken-siseki.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref> | location=|latd=36|latm=16|lats=29.8 | longd=138|longm=28|longs=55.9 | map=Japan Nagano | label=Ōi Castle | latitude_deg=36|latitude_min=16|latitude_sec=29.8 | longitude_deg=138|longitude_min=28|longitude_sec=55.9 | icon=Japanese castle ruins | label_position=bottom }}
'''Ōi Castle''' (大井城, ''Ōi-jō'') was a [[:en:Japanese castle|Japanese castle]] located in [[Iwamurada]], [[Saku City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a Nagano Prefecture-designated [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] and was the seat of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as deputies to the [[Governor of Shinano Province]]. The castle was also known as '''Iwamurada Residence''' (岩村田館).
== History ==
Ōi Castle was located in northeastern Saku City, along the [[Yukawa River (Nagano)|Yukawa]]'s cliffs. It consisted of three fortifications: Ishinarabi Castle (石並城), Ōjō (王城), and Kuroiwa Castle (黒岩城). According to the ''[[Shirin Danzō]]'', the castle was established by {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a branch of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] descending from [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagachika]]. From its founding in [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221) until its fall in [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), the Ōi clan controlled northeastern Saku District from this base.
In [[Kōan (era)|Kōan]] 2 (1279), the second lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}} donated a temple bell to the {{ill|Shinzenkō-ji|ja|新善光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenkō-ji|1=Shinzenkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501510}} in [[:en:Naruse|Naruse]], Saku. The exact location of the manor depicted in the ''Illustrated Biography of Ippen'' remains uncertain.
During the [[:en:Kemmu Restoration|Kemmu Restoration]] in [[Kemmu (era)|Kemmu]] 2 (1335), the fourth lord {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}} supported the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], joining the Northern Court. He clashed with the Southern Court forces, including {{ill|Kutsuna Shigekiyo|ja|忽那重清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuna Shigekiyo|1=Kutsuna Shigekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56253681}}, who were advancing toward [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]] along the Eastern Mountain Road. Despite several days of fierce resistance, the castle eventually fell, as recorded in documents like ''Battle of Ōi Manor'' and ''Kutsuna Papers''.
The castle was later rebuilt. The fifth lord, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, served as Deputy Governor of Shinano Province. His son, the sixth lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, mediated during the [[Ōtō Rebellion]] in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 7 (1400), aiding the defeated [[:en:Ogasawara Nagahide|Ogasawara Nagahide]].
Under the seventh lord {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, the clan reached its zenith. The Ōi castle town flourished, with over 6,000 households, surpassing the provincial capital ([[:en:Matsumoto City|Matsumoto]]). Mochimitsu's reign saw vast territorial control, including 60,000 ''koku'' in northern Saku and estates in [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke]] and [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi]]. He reportedly led 1,000 troops to Kyoto.
In [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] 10 (1438), Mochimitsu sheltered {{ill|Eijū ōmaru|ja|永寿王丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eijū ōmaru|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the orphaned son of [[:en:Ashikaga Mochiuji|Ashikaga Mochiuji]], at {{ill|An'yō-ji|ja|安養寺 (佐久市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'yō-ji|1=An'yō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043045}} during the [[Eikyō Rebellion]].
Subsequent lords faced difficulties. In [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), {{ill|Murakami Masakiyo|ja|村上政清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Masakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} attacked Ōi Castle, which was set ablaze, marking the end of the Ōi main family’s 260-year rule.
Though the main line perished, branches like the {{ill|Iwao clan|ja|岩尾氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwao clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Aiki clan|ja|相木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} persisted. {{ill|Ōi Genkei|ja|大井玄慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Genkei|1=Ōi Haruyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920475}} inherited the castle but was ousted by [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] in [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 12 (1543). Later, during the [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] era, {{ill|Ōi Gakujo|ja|大井雅楽助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Gakujo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrendered the castle to {{ill|Yoda Nobushige|ja|依田信蕃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoda Nobushige|1=Yoda Nobushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385519}}, a retainer of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]].
Today, little remains of the castle. The main enclosure is a public park, home to a giant [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]] designated a prefectural natural monument<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Natural Monuments|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/15ken-tennenkinenbutu.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
== Bibliography == * ''{{ill|Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|ja|日本城郭大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|1=Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507767}}'' Vol. 8, Nagano-Yamanashi, 1980 * Masaharu Sasamoto, ''The Two Battles of Kawanakajima'', 1998 * Hisako Tanabe, ''Uesugi Norinaga'', 1999 * Yuki Sakata, ''Kantō Kubō Ashikaga Nariuji'', 2004 * Shinobu Ōi, ''Tales of the Ōi Clan'', 2005 * Toshio Ōi, ''Chronicles of the Iwao Family'' (Modern Japanese Translation), 1996
== External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www1.ocn.ne.jp/~oomi/sakugun.html|title=History of the Saku Area – Prosperity of Iwamurada|date=2015-07-02}} * {{Wayback|url=http://rarememory.justhpbs.jp/ooi/ooi.htm|title=Rise and Fall of the Ōi Clan|date=2012-05-02}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ju8t-hnm/Shiro/TokaiKoshin/Nagano/Ooi/index.html|title=Ōi Castle: Buried Fortresses|date=2016-03-04}}

| build_year = [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221) | rulers = {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}}, {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, {{ill|Ōi Awa-maru|ja|大井安房丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Awa-maru|1=Ōi Awamaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22124423}}, among others | destroyed_year = [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) | remains = Kuruwa (enclosures) | cultural_asset = Nagano Prefecture Historic Site<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Historic Sites|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/13ken-siseki.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref> | location=|latd=36|latm=16|lats=29.8 | longd=138|longm=28|longs=55.9 | map=Japan Nagano | label=Ōi Castle | latitude_deg=36|latitude_min=16|latitude_sec=29.8 | longitude_deg=138|longitude_min=28|longitude_sec=55.9 | icon=Japanese castle ruins | label_position=bottom }}
'''Ōi Castle''' (大井城, ''Ōi-jō'') was a [[:en:Japanese castle|Japanese castle]] located in [[Iwamurada]], [[Saku City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. It is a Nagano Prefecture-designated [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] and was the seat of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as deputies to the [[Governor of Shinano Province]]. The castle was also known as '''Iwamurada Residence''' (岩村田館).
== History ==
Ōi Castle was located in northeastern Saku City, along the [[Yukawa River (Nagano)|Yukawa]]'s cliffs. It consisted of three fortifications: Ishinarabi Castle (石並城), Ōjō (王城), and Kuroiwa Castle (黒岩城). According to the ''[[Shirin Danzō]]'', the castle was established by {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a branch of the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] descending from [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagachika]]. From its founding in [[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 3 (1221) until its fall in [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), the Ōi clan controlled northeastern Saku District from this base.
In [[Kōan (era)|Kōan]] 2 (1279), the second lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}} donated a temple bell to the {{ill|Shinzenkō-ji|ja|新善光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenkō-ji|1=Shinzenkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501510}} in [[:en:Naruse|Naruse]], Saku. The exact location of the manor depicted in the ''Illustrated Biography of Ippen'' remains uncertain.
During the [[:en:Kemmu Restoration|Kemmu Restoration]] in [[Kemmu (era)|Kemmu]] 2 (1335), the fourth lord {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}} supported the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga clan]], joining the Northern Court. He clashed with the Southern Court forces, including {{ill|Kutsuna Shigekiyo|ja|忽那重清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuna Shigekiyo|1=Kutsuna Shigekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56253681}}, who were advancing toward [[:en:Kamakura|Kamakura]] along the Eastern Mountain Road. Despite several days of fierce resistance, the castle eventually fell, as recorded in documents like ''Battle of Ōi Manor'' and ''Kutsuna Papers''.
The castle was later rebuilt. The fifth lord, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, served as Deputy Governor of Shinano Province. His son, the sixth lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, mediated during the [[Ōtō Rebellion]] in [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 7 (1400), aiding the defeated [[:en:Ogasawara Nagahide|Ogasawara Nagahide]].
Under the seventh lord {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, the clan reached its zenith. The Ōi castle town flourished, with over 6,000 households, surpassing the provincial capital ([[:en:Matsumoto City|Matsumoto]]). Mochimitsu's reign saw vast territorial control, including 60,000 ''koku'' in northern Saku and estates in [[:en:Kōzuke Province|Kōzuke]] and [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi]]. He reportedly led 1,000 troops to Kyoto.
In [[:en:Eikyō|Eikyō]] 10 (1438), Mochimitsu sheltered {{ill|Eijū ōmaru|ja|永寿王丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eijū ōmaru|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the orphaned son of [[:en:Ashikaga Mochiuji|Ashikaga Mochiuji]], at {{ill|An'yō-ji|ja|安養寺 (佐久市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'yō-ji|1=An'yō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043045}} during the [[Eikyō Rebellion]].
Subsequent lords faced difficulties. In [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), {{ill|Murakami Masakiyo|ja|村上政清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Masakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} attacked Ōi Castle, which was set ablaze, marking the end of the Ōi main family’s 260-year rule.
Though the main line perished, branches like the {{ill|Iwao clan|ja|岩尾氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwao clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Aiki clan|ja|相木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} persisted. {{ill|Ōi Genkei|ja|大井玄慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Genkei|1=Ōi Haruyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920475}} inherited the castle but was ousted by [[:en:Takeda Shingen|Takeda Shingen]] in [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 12 (1543). Later, during the [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] era, {{ill|Ōi Gakujo|ja|大井雅楽助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Gakujo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrendered the castle to {{ill|Yoda Nobushige|ja|依田信蕃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoda Nobushige|1=Yoda Nobushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385519}}, a retainer of [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]].
Today, little remains of the castle. The main enclosure is a public park, home to a giant [[:en:Zelkova|Zelkova]] designated a prefectural natural monument<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Natural Monuments|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/15ken-tennenkinenbutu.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
== Bibliography == * ''{{ill|Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|ja|日本城郭大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|1=Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507767}}'' Vol. 8, Nagano-Yamanashi, 1980 * Masaharu Sasamoto, ''The Two Battles of Kawanakajima'', 1998 * Hisako Tanabe, ''Uesugi Norinaga'', 1999 * Yuki Sakata, ''Kantō Kubō Ashikaga Nariuji'', 2004 * Shinobu Ōi, ''Tales of the Ōi Clan'', 2005 * Toshio Ōi, ''Chronicles of the Iwao Family'' (Modern Japanese Translation), 1996
== External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www1.ocn.ne.jp/~oomi/sakugun.html|title=History of the Saku Area – Prosperity of Iwamurada|date=2015-07-02}} * {{Wayback|url=http://rarememory.justhpbs.jp/ooi/ooi.htm|title=Rise and Fall of the Ōi Clan|date=2012-05-02}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ju8t-hnm/Shiro/TokaiKoshin/Nagano/Ooi/index.html|title=Ōi Castle: Buried Fortresses|date=2016-03-04}}

| build_year = {{ill|Jōkyū|en|Jōkyū|WD=Q1195428|lt=Jōkyū}} 3 (1221) | rulers = {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}}, {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, {{ill|Ōi Awa-maru|ja|大井安房丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Awa-maru|1=Ōi Awamaru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22124423}}, among others | destroyed_year = [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484) | remains = Kuruwa (enclosures) | cultural_asset = Nagano Prefecture Historic Site<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Historic Sites|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/13ken-siseki.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref> | location=|latd=36|latm=16|lats=29.8 | longd=138|longm=28|longs=55.9 | map=Japan Nagano | label=Ōi Castle | latitude_deg=36|latitude_min=16|latitude_sec=29.8 | longitude_deg=138|longitude_min=28|longitude_sec=55.9 | icon=Japanese castle ruins | label_position=bottom }}
'''Ōi Castle''' (大井城, ''Ōi-jō'') was a {{ill|Japanese castle|en|Japanese castle|WD=Q92026|lt=Japanese castle}} located in [[Iwamurada]], [[Saku City]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is a Nagano Prefecture-designated [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] and was the seat of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served as deputies to the [[Governor of Shinano Province]]. The castle was also known as '''Iwamurada Residence''' (岩村田館).
== History ==
Ōi Castle was located in northeastern Saku City, along the [[Yukawa River (Nagano)|Yukawa]]'s cliffs. It consisted of three fortifications: Ishinarabi Castle (石並城), Ōjō (王城), and Kuroiwa Castle (黒岩城). According to the ''[[Shirin Danzō]]'', the castle was established by {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja|信濃大井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi clan (Shinano Province)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a branch of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji}} descending from {{ill|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|en|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|WD=Q7079829|lt=Ogasawara Nagachika}}. From its founding in {{ill|Jōkyū|en|Jōkyū|WD=Q1195428|lt=Jōkyū}} 3 (1221) until its fall in [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), the Ōi clan controlled northeastern Saku District from this base.
In [[Kōan (era)|Kōan]] 2 (1279), the second lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunaga|ja|大井光長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunaga|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432140}} donated a temple bell to the {{ill|Shinzenkō-ji|ja|新善光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzenkō-ji|1=Shinzenkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501510}} in {{ill|Naruse|en|Naruse|WD=Q21513057|lt=Naruse}}, Saku. The exact location of the manor depicted in the ''Illustrated Biography of Ippen'' remains uncertain.
During the [[:en:Kemmu Restoration|Kemmu Restoration]] in [[Kemmu (era)|Kemmu]] 2 (1335), the fourth lord {{ill|Ōi Chikayuki|ja|大井朝行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Chikayuki|1=Ōi Tomoyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432189}} supported the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga clan}}, joining the Northern Court. He clashed with the Southern Court forces, including {{ill|Kutsuna Shigekiyo|ja|忽那重清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kutsuna Shigekiyo|1=Kutsuna Shigekiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56253681}}, who were advancing toward {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|WD=Q200267|lt=Kamakura}} along the Eastern Mountain Road. Despite several days of fierce resistance, the castle eventually fell, as recorded in documents like ''Battle of Ōi Manor'' and ''Kutsuna Papers''.
The castle was later rebuilt. The fifth lord, {{ill|Ōi Mitsusaka|ja|大井光栄|lt=Ōi Mitsunaga (Mitsusaka)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsusaka|1=Ōi Mitsunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432137}}, served as Deputy Governor of Shinano Province. His son, the sixth lord {{ill|Ōi Mitsunori|ja|大井光矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mitsunori|1=Ōi Mitsunori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432141}}, mediated during the [[Ōtō Rebellion]] in {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei}} 7 (1400), aiding the defeated {{ill|Ogasawara Nagahide|en|Ogasawara Nagahide|WD=Q7079827|lt=Ogasawara Nagahide}}.
Under the seventh lord {{ill|Ōi Mochimitsu|ja|大井持光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Mochimitsu|1=Ōi Mochimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17220996}}, the clan reached its zenith. The Ōi castle town flourished, with over 6,000 households, surpassing the provincial capital ([[:en:Matsumoto City|Matsumoto]]). Mochimitsu's reign saw vast territorial control, including 60,000 ''koku'' in northern Saku and estates in {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|WD=Q1054838|lt=Kōzuke}} and {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi}}. He reportedly led 1,000 troops to Kyoto.
In {{ill|Eikyō|en|Eikyō|WD=Q1193059|lt=Eikyō}} 10 (1438), Mochimitsu sheltered {{ill|Eijū ōmaru|ja|永寿王丸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eijū ōmaru|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the orphaned son of {{ill|Ashikaga Mochiuji|en|Ashikaga Mochiuji|WD=Q4805073|lt=Ashikaga Mochiuji}}, at {{ill|An'yō-ji|ja|安養寺 (佐久市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'yō-ji|1=An'yō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20043045}} during the [[Eikyō Rebellion]].
Subsequent lords faced difficulties. In [[Bunmei (era)|Bunmei]] 16 (1484), {{ill|Murakami Masakiyo|ja|村上政清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Masakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} attacked Ōi Castle, which was set ablaze, marking the end of the Ōi main family’s 260-year rule.
Though the main line perished, branches like the {{ill|Iwao clan|ja|岩尾氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwao clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Aiki clan|ja|相木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aiki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} persisted. {{ill|Ōi Genkei|ja|大井玄慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Genkei|1=Ōi Haruyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920475}} inherited the castle but was ousted by {{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|WD=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen}} in {{ill|Tenbun|en|Tenbun|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 12 (1543). Later, during the [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] era, {{ill|Ōi Gakujo|ja|大井雅楽助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Gakujo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrendered the castle to {{ill|Yoda Nobushige|ja|依田信蕃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoda Nobushige|1=Yoda Nobushige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385519}}, a retainer of {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}.
Today, little remains of the castle. The main enclosure is a public park, home to a giant {{ill|Zelkova|en|Zelkova|WD=Q187454|lt=Zelkova}} designated a prefectural natural monument<ref>{{Cite web|title=Prefectural Natural Monuments|url=http://www.pref.nagano.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunsho/bunka/rekishi/documents/15ken-tennenkinenbutu.pdf|format=PDF|publisher=Nagano Prefecture|date=2016-02-25|accessdate=2016-09-09}}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist}} {{Citation help}}
== Bibliography == * ''{{ill|Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|ja|日本城郭大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|1=Japanese Castle Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507767}}'' Vol. 8, Nagano-Yamanashi, 1980 * Masaharu Sasamoto, ''The Two Battles of Kawanakajima'', 1998 * Hisako Tanabe, ''Uesugi Norinaga'', 1999 * Yuki Sakata, ''Kantō Kubō Ashikaga Nariuji'', 2004 * Shinobu Ōi, ''Tales of the Ōi Clan'', 2005 * Toshio Ōi, ''Chronicles of the Iwao Family'' (Modern Japanese Translation), 1996
== External Links == * {{Wayback|url=http://www1.ocn.ne.jp/~oomi/sakugun.html|title=History of the Saku Area – Prosperity of Iwamurada|date=2015-07-02}} * {{Wayback|url=http://rarememory.justhpbs.jp/ooi/ooi.htm|title=Rise and Fall of the Ōi Clan|date=2012-05-02}} * {{Wayback|url=http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ju8t-hnm/Shiro/TokaiKoshin/Nagano/Ooi/index.html|title=Ōi Castle: Buried Fortresses|date=2016-03-04}}
